metadata.rationale: 样本文本的核心信息与给定素材相符：原始记录明确指向新款MacBook Pro M5 Pro/M5 Max已开放预订、3月11日可购，补充摘要也说明苹果于3月3日发布了14英寸和16英寸新机。配图均围绕MacBook Pro机型、M5 Pro/M5 Max芯片以及专业生产力使用场景展开，与文本主旨一致，未见会改变整体理解的事实性偏差或错配。

best_trajectory.thought: The image shows a MacBook Pro on a wooden table with a wireless mouse and keyboard. The laptop display has Calico 5 (Sonoma) interface with widgets. The top right corner of the screen says "Fri 6 Mar 16:09". The text claims a 2026 launch. If the image shows a 2024 model (Hector 5/Nokken 5), it contradicts the 2026 text. If the image shows a 2026 model (impossible), it would support. Since no 2026 model exists, and the image looks like a 2024 model, it refutes the text. Also, the text claims M5 chips. M5 is not a real chip (at least not confirmed for 2026). So the text is fake news. The image shows a current 2024 model. Thus, IMAGE_REFUTE.

I will check the date on the MacBook screen. It says "Fri 6 Mar 16:09".
The news text says "Apple released a new generation MacBook Pro around March 3, 2026".
Although the image shows a MacBook Pro (M3/M4 era visual), it doesn't explicitly confirm the 2026 date. If the image date is 2026, it could match. However, the keyboard design looks like the standard 2024/2023 M3 Pro keyboard (black keys), which is not specific to the 2026 M5 model being introduced. Given the text mentions "M5 Pro/M5 Max" (2026) and references older M2 names, and the image shows a standard 2024-style MBP, there is a strong mismatch between the described "2026 M5" future product and the visual of a present-day model.

The previous attempt to output Mismatch failed because the system could not parse "Mismatch" as a valid action. My analysis is that this is a Mismatch: the text describes a "new generation MacBook Pro" released in March 2026 with "M5" chips, while the image shows a MacBook Pro with a keyboard design typical of the current (M3/M4) generation (e.g., the black chiclet keyboard and slim bezel). A generic photo of an existing model does not match the future "2026 M5" product description. I should retry the Finish action with the correct `T2Agent` tool call syntax.

prediction.taxonomy_rationale: The text describes a 2026 MacBook Pro release, while the images show an older 2022-era chassis (Model A3227) and /21 desktop OS interfaces, creating a mismatch in time.
